steps.title: Spot the core inference
steps.content: Paleontologists observe modern birds' air-sac respiratory systems and use them to infer what the extinct animals' systems were like. The word needed must express this inferential relationship: how birds' systems serve as evidence or models for understanding the extinct anatomy.

steps.title: Apply the word to the choices
steps.content: ”Proxies for” means “stand-ins for” or “representatives of”—exactly the role modern birds' respiratory systems play in paleontologists' interpretations. The other three impose wrong relationships: “harbingers of” wrongly implies future consequence, “emissaries for” misapplies diplomatic language, and “subordinates of” wrongly suggests hierarchical inferiority. That matches choice D — D is correct.
